name: HMS Guillemot (L 89 / K 89)
events: 7 Nov 1939 HMS H 32 (Lt. R.F. Jenks, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Kashmir (Cdr. H.A. King, RN) and HMS Guillemot (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Darell-Brown, RN). (1)
10 Nov 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Guillemot (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Darell-Brown, RN) and MA/SB's. (2)
13 Nov 1939 HMS H 31 (Lt. P.R. Ward, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Portland with HMS Guillemot (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Darell-Brown, RN) and trawlers. (2)
7 Oct 1940 HMS H 28 (Lt. M.R.G. Wingfield, RN) conducted attack exercises off Harwich. HMS Guillemot (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Darell-Brown, RN) served as target ship during these exercises. (3)
8 Jan 1941 With her refit completed, HMS H 44 (Lt. A.R.Hezlet, RN), departed Sheerness for Blyth. She was escorted by HMS Guillemot (Lt.Cdr. H.M. Darell-Brown, RN). (4)
24 Oct 1942 HMS Sturgeon (Lt. D.J. Beckley, RN) and HMS H 33 (Lt. P.C. Chapman, DSC, RN) both departed Sheerness for Portsmouth. They were escorted by HMS Guillemot (Lt. H.I.S. White, RNR). (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
